name: Swedish Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak bread crumbs in milk until softened.
2. Add onion to 1 tablespoon butter in a small saucepan and cook slowly for 3 minutes.
3. Mix the softened bread, cooked onion, and meat together.
4. Add eggs, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and paprika; mix thoroughly until very smooth and light.
5. Shape the mixture into small balls, dusting your hands with flour while shaping.
6. Melt remaining 3 tablespoons butter in a large frying pan.
7. Add meatballs and fry until golden brown on all sides.
8. Remove from the pan and stir in the meat extract and 3 tablespoons flour until well blended.
9. Add water, a few grains of pepper; cook, stirring constantly until thickened.
10. Reduce heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
11. Stir in sour cream, a rounded tablespoon at a time, stirring until thoroughly blended after each addition.
12. Return meatballs to the gravy, cover the pan, and simmer very gently for 10 minutes.
13. Serve in deep casserole, sprinkled with minced parsley. Yield: 70 to 80 meatballs.
